text.title: Outcome-Based Pricing Output Contract
text.summary: Pins the shape of the pricing memo per prospect and the saved calculator state. Founder must complete this BEFORE the proposal call.

field.name: outcome.prospect_stated_baseline
field.notes: numeric value + unit + measurement window + verbatim quote

field.name: outcome.target_lift_conservative
field.notes: numeric value with quote-citation or industry-benchmark citation

forbidden.pattern: outcome.prospect_stated_baseline missing the verbatim quote (industry-benchmark substituted for prospect's number)
confidence.score >= 4 AND confidence.portfolio_evidence has fewer than 3 case study IDs
capture.rate > 0.15 AND no written guarantee / success-fee structure in the contract
asking_to_floor_ratio < 1.6 (no negotiation headroom; deal is not worth pursuing as outcome-based)
discount_ladder step is a pure price cut with no tradeoff (every step must trade away scope, terms, or exclusivity)
floor.committed_value_usd edited after the proposal call (floor must be pre-committed)

allowed-transformations.transformation: Round asking_price to the nearest $500 below $20k, nearest $1k above; never to suspicious precision like $48,750.
Compress the confidence justification to a one-paragraph evidence list when presenting to prospect; keep the full data in the memo.
Re-express outcome.annual_value as monthly when the prospect's mental model is monthly; never as both in the same proposal page.

list.item: Outcome value cites the prospect's own quote, not an industry benchmark alone
Confidence score backed by named portfolio case studies
Capture rate within the band for the engagement type
Floor committed in writing BEFORE the call
Each discount-ladder step trades something concrete, not just price
